chapter_title.en: Chapter 107 – The merits for the poor Muslims

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (359/ 9)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرفوع - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (359/ 9)
text: قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) مَا أُعْطِيَ عَبْدٌ مِنَ الدُّنْيَا إِلَّا اعْتِبَاراً وَ مَا زُوِيَ عَنْهُ إِلَّا اخْتِبَاراً .

en.hubeali: From him, from one of his companions, raising it, said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whatever Given to a servant from the world is only as a lesson, and whatever is Delayed from him is only as a Trial’.
en.sarwar: 6. It is narrated from him (narrator of the Hadith above) from certain individuals of his people in a marfu’ manner from abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) who has said the following: “Whatever a servant is given of worldly things is to make him learn a lesson and whatever is withheld from him is to place him under the trial.’”
